entry.title: Re: SAP DRC automation public cloud Netherlands
entry.content:

Thank you very much for your reply and the step by step approach to load it.

Regarding testing: Then there is a "test run" option to test the connection?

KR,

Danny

entry.title: Re: Configure Governance Rules in SAP Build
entry.content:
Hi @Sidney ,
yes it'll work if you already have Cloud Transport Management setup done for transport, please follow the steps written in this blog, will help to setup governance rules.
Regards,
Diganta

entry.title: Re: Configure Governance Rules in SAP Build
entry.content:
Hi @sshrey14 ,

If you want to do governance rule setup for transport, first you need to setup Cloud Transport Management system for transport the project from one system to other, then you can follow this blog to enable governance rule.

Governance rules are configured at the tenant level. Once the setup is completed for a tenant, it is automatically applicable to all projects within that tenant.
Regards,
Diganta

entry.title: Re: Three new courses for SAP Business One, Web Client are now live on learning.sap.com
entry.content:
Hi Community,

Are those courses replacing the old TB1000, TB1100 and TB1200?

What about those people who were trained in those courses for ##certification? Should they start the new courses from scratch?

entry.title: When running the Simplification Item Check, a consistency check result error appears as shown below.
entry.content:
When running the Simplification Item Check, a consistency check result error appears as shown below. How can we resolve this and proceed? Screenshots are attached.

Check Sub-ID:"DO_SDMI_CHECK->CL_NGC_CORE_SDM_MSEHI_CABN", return code = 12 .
The SDMI (Silent Data Migration) CL_NGC_CORE_SDM_MSEHI_CABN is still initial in client
000. There are 11  relevant entries still to be migrated.
Error: The SDMI CL_NGC_CORE_SDM_MSEHI_CABN is not uptime-capable on the next target
release.
Therefore the SDMI must finish before the upgrade in all clients mentioned above.
The SDMI is registered for S4CORE, current version: 105  , last_uptime_release: 106  .
Target release:108
Explanation "Initial": If the status remains "Initial" over a long time (days) then this
means that there might be a problem with the background job for SDMI execution.
You can check the logs for possible issues (SDM-MON -> Display application log). Normally
the SDMIs should either go to status F (finished) or N (not relevant).
For questions please open a message to the component of the SDMI class CA-CL.
The following select statement can be used to see the relevant data for migration: (check
in correct client)
SELECT * FROM PSDMMSEHICABN WHERE SDM_STATUS < '02'

entry.title: Re: Permission Issue

entry.title: Re: SAP SF Compensation - Best Practice Workflow without...

Best Regards,
Joachim

entry.title: Loading Master and Transactional Data from Datasphere into SAC - Seamless Planing
entry.content:
Hi,

i have created a planning model in SAC, which is also stored and managed in SAP Datasphere.

To load master data and transactional data from Datasphere views into my model, the only method I have found so far is to create OData service connections.

Since I’m working with multiple dimensions, this approach results in a large number of individual connections being visible in SAC, which significantly reduces clarity and usability.

My questions:

  1. Is this currently the only way to integrate master and transactional data from Datasphere views into a SAC planning model that is also stored in Datasphere?

  2. In the context of Seamless Planning, would this OData-based approach be considered appropriate – or are there best practices that suggest otherwise?

Thank you in advance for your support and feedback.

entry.title: Re: CAP Deployment - Error on starting DB Deployer App
entry.content: Hello, were you able to solve this? I am new here and am facing the new error ;-;

entry.title: Re: TCS Configuration for Scrap Sales in India (SAP Publi...
entry.content: For TCS threshold validation, check SAP note 3100340.

entry.title: Re: Mandatory document attachment tab in Manage Certifica...
entry.content:
Hello @RishikeshMishra 

In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, attaching a document in the Manage Certificate Receipts app is optional; there’s no system control to make attachments mandatory before setting the status to "Received."

If your client needs this as a strict requirement, an SAP Influence Request would be needed.

Check below
SAP Help – Quality Certificate Receipt Process

Let me know if you need further assistance

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Overall Performance Rating for Compensation Module - Standard Process
entry.content:
Dear all,

Do the SAP SuccessFactors standard processes specify that the Compensation module must use the Overall Performance Rating that is displayed in the Talent Profile, or does the standard also allow the Compensation module to use an Overall Performance Rating of a different performance form?
So, do the standard processes allow that different Performance Ratings can be used in different modules, or do the different modules have to use the rating, which is displayed in the talent profile?

Many thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: Couldn't create an instance of service 'ABAP environment' with plan 'shared'.
entry.content: Thanks a lot @paulgiesa, now i've the abap environment instance created 🙂

entry.title: Re: sap public cloud release of2508
entry.content:
Hello @jerrysam 

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, including the latest 2508 release, you can track changes by module like Sourcing & Procurement using:

What's New Viewer – Provides release-specific updates by module, including detailed changes

Roadmap Explorer – Shows upcoming innovations by product and module
https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=S4HANA_CLOUD

Test Your Processes App. In your tenant, this helps identify changes based on your active scope items.

You can filter by scope items or specific modules to track only relevant updates for your project.

Let me know if you need help navigating these tools.

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: New SAP Design System Portal for SAP Fiori Design Guidelines, and New UI Kits for Designers

entry.title: IP-Based Allow-Listing for Applications
entry.content:
For sensitive applications it's often advisable to follow a defense in depth approach. By layering multiple indepenent security controls the risk of a successful attack can be reduced to a minimum.

When the clients that are allowed to connect to a system are well-known and have fixed IP address ranges (such as from a corporate VPN service), applications can first filter for these IP addresses. This is usually done by implementing network ACLs or a firewall that is dedicated to filtering these requests before forwarding them.

In combination with strong authentication like mTLS, this can significantly reduce the risk of a successful attack. You should never rely on IP-based allow-listing alone to protect any resource.

Introducing X-CF-True-Client-IP Header

Up until now this was a difficult task when running as an application on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment as the IP address of the client is not readily available to the app. When a client connects to the platform, it connects to a central load-balancer which handles (among other things) the termination of the TLS connection. When the request reaches the application, the connection is initiated from the load-balancer which uses an internal IP address that cannot be used for such traffic filtering. This gets even more complicated when route-services are in use.

To make building custom filters as easy as possible we have introduced a new request header which will be set in every reqeusts sent to applications: x-cf-true-client-ip. This header contains a single IP address (IPv4 or IPv6) that was used to connect to the platform when the request was initiated.

When the application has a route-service configured, the reported IP address will be the same for the route service and the application, always pointing to the original client which initiated the request.

How to Use X-CF-True-Client-IP

There are two main options to implementing IP-based allow-listing using x-cf-true-client-ip:

  • directly in the main application logic, or
  • in a route service which is then bound to the application route.

In either case you will need to load a pre-defined allow-list, and for every request retrieve the IP from the header and check it against the configured allow-list. To demonstrate the functionality we have implemented a sample route-service with a dummy-app as backend:

Sample Application Using Route-Services

To demonstrate this, we have added two sample applications to our cf-routing-samples. One is the route service itself at ip-allow-listing-route-service/ and the second one is a dummy to act as the protected backend at ip-allow-listing-route-service/ok/.

Deploying the applications can be done via the CF CLI and provided manifest:

# First, clone the repository.
git clone https://github.com/SAP-samples/cf-routing-samples
cd cf-routing-samples/ip-allow-listing-route-service
# Login to CF.
cf login [...]
cf target -o <org> -s <space>
# Push the applications.
cf push --var domain=<domain> --var suffix=<suffix> -f ./manifest.yml

Make sure to replace <domain> with the domain of your environment and provide a unique suffix for <suffix> to prevent collisions on the name of the route. Eventually the CLI should report success and you should see your running applications with 'cf apps'. To ensure our target application works, you can use curl:

curl https://ok-<suffix>.cfapps.eu12.hana.ondemand.com

This should print 'ok'. To protect our ok application with the allow-listing route-service we still need to configure them together. The first step is to turn the allow-listing app into a route-service we can bind to. A route service intercepts requests to an app and can decide whether to forward a request to the actual app or not. We use this mechanism to block requests that are originating from a source that is not in the allow-list.

cf create-user-provided-service allow-listing -r https://ip-allow-list-rs-<suffix>.cfapps.eu12.hana.ondemand.com

Make sure to provide the same suffix here as you did when pushing the application. Now we can bind the route service to the route of our target application:

cf bind-route-service <domain> --hostname ok-<suffix> allow-listing

To view the finished setup we can use the 'cf routes' command which will output something like this:

space     host                        domain     port   path   protocol   app-protocol   apps                             service instance   options
<space>   ip-allow-list-rs-<suffix>   <domain>                 http       http1          ip-allow-listing-route-service
<space>   ok-<suffix>                 <domain>                 http       http1          ok                               allow-listing

By default, the route-service will allow all traffic from every IPv4 address, so accessing the application should still work:

curl -4 https://ok-<suffix>.cfapps.eu12.hana.ondemand.com

If you now delete the single line from the allow-list and push the app again using the same command as before (no need to re-do the service, that persists), you will no longer be able to access it:

error: forbidden: address '192.0.2.5' is not in allow-list

Feel free to take a look at the implementation but please note that this code is only for demonstration purposes and must not be used in production / live systems.

There are some pointers in the code comments on what to do differently, but as always use good judgement and security best practices. The mechanism, by which to provide the specific allow-list to the route service is also left as exercise to the reader.

References

  • SAP-samples/cf-routing-samples
  • Cloud Foundry Route Services

entry.title: Re: newcomer student
entry.content:
Hey Younes,

I’m so glad to hear that you’re interested in starting a local SAP Community group in Casablanca, that’s fantastic news!

To get started, I recommend checking out our Become an Event Organizer page.

It explains what’s involved and how we support you through every step of the way. If you have any questions while going through it, feel free to reach out. I’m happy to help!

Looking forward to seeing the SAP Community grow in Casablanca!

entry.title: Re: Error when trying to delete fs item
entry.content: Thanks for the suggestion, however when we tried to implement it, we got the message that this note cannot be implemented into our system.

entry.title: Re: sap public cloud release of2508
entry.content:
Hello Chuma ,

 

Thankyou for prompt response  , as I struggled to use these things OR links  how can we filter the scopes that are existing as per our activated ones that would be great to know what we use these links as per our business scope

entry.title: Re: SAP Java Buildpack 2: Jakarta-Based, Fresh and New!
entry.content:
Another question:

As part of moving from Docker based to Buildpack based deployment, we are setting extra JAVA options (startup parameters) and security configurations that were set for Docker java applications.

One of the security configurations is networkaddress.cache.ttl.

This was done this way in the Dockerfile of the JDK:

RUN echo "networkaddress.cache.ttl = 30" >> ${JAVA_HOME}/conf/security/java.security

 

What would be the corresponding way to add this security configuration to sap_java_buildpack_jakarta ?

Note: it cannot be set with -D option. This has no effect.

entry.title: Re: Sudden wrong results in app behaviour for previous well working formulas in settings and compone
entry.content:
@timokapanen 

Dear Timo,

I put a timer of 2 seconds on app launch for the screen orientation data variable. it works on SAP Preview and on the app. 

Concerning the sounds written to file (base 64 encoding) written to local folder on app launch, the SAP Preview which a few days ago was not working (not producing sounds) is now producing the sounds without need of adding a delay on app launch but the built app on Test Flight is not producing the sounds for iphone XR, 11 Pro, Ipad M1 and iphone 16 (all models tested).

 It looks like the build encoder is not going through stop points like first going to create all app variables and then loading then only after this first step.  So maybe the app building engine (to get the zip.gpg and then the ipa file) need to be rechecked so that the correct building steps are going one after the other and that for example no uncreated app variable is already trying to be fed a value in the resulting ipa app file. What is strange is that it was working before.

Also it would be weird on programming side to try to guess a delay value so that system is not overwhelmed, it would be a substantial inner fragility of the app building (encoding) system.

Thank you for checking

Best regards,

Chantal

entry.title: Associated GL Company - FI Company
entry.content: When creating consolidation units in Group Reporting, the companies that appear as SAP are the “associated GL companies” and not the FI companies. Could you help me understand why this is happening?

entry.title: Re: Auto printing issue while adding marketting documents
entry.content: Can you explain me how to redirect to virtual printer?.I am probably thinking page setup settings in Crystal report does not affect anything.And how to check for scripts in crystal report.I have check formula fields and nothing related to printing was mentioned

entry.title: Re: Recruiting Groups per legal entity
entry.content:
Hi @alejandroazca

 

Set Permission Rules:
    • Adjust the Permission Rules so that each Recruiting Group has access restrictions based on legal entity identifiers.
    • Configure permission roles in “Admin Center” -> “Manage Permission Roles”, and define the criteria using either a custom field (representing legal entity) or leveraging existing fields like "Department" or "Location".

How can this step be done? In the Role Assignments there is no way to set target criteria = job requisition.legal entity

how can we restrict only by Job Requisition legal entity / BU?

entry.title: Re: Unnecessary objects are visible while applying Leave...
entry.content: Okay, Thank you Arun

entry.title: Re: Error installation ECC6 Ehp7 Windows
entry.content: We are currently experiencing this error as well. How did you resolve it？

entry.title: Re: sap public cloud release of2508
entry.content:
Hello @jerrysam 

Good follow-up, to filter the updates based on your activated scopes, you can use:

As I previously mentioned, the 'Test Your Processes' app in your system displays the scope items you’ve activated and helps track changes or test impacts. In the What’s New Viewer, filter by Scope Items if you know their IDs (e.g., 1XV, 3VX); your project team or CBC setup should provide your scope list.

Unfortunately, there’s no automatic link between What’s New Viewer and your system’s scopes, so manual filtering by known scope items is needed.

If this answer to your question, please accept as the solution, otherwise feel free to contact me anytime

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Introducing Generative AI in SAP SuccessFactors Hands-On Practice Systems
entry.content:
Hello @Markus777 ,

Yes! 🎉 We are currently working on implementing Joule in SuccessFactors practice systems and it will be available on learning.sap.com very soon. 🙂

Regards,

Roxana

entry.title: Re: SAP TechEd 2025: Registration is live – check out our brand new hybrid experience!
entry.content: We are one big happy family @TammyPowlas 😀 - hope to meet you in person some day.

entry.title: Re: This is regarding Poland e-invoicing, Is there any fi...
entry.content:
Note 3279206 - Electronic Document Processing for Poland - KSeF: Implementation Overview basically stipulates that 'You must subscribe to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition service to exchange electronic documents with the tax authority (KSeF). Please get in touch with your SAP representative for further details.'

  • Without GRC, it seems that this would require either custom integration or the use of third-party middleware.
  • What's your version/type of SAP system; S/4HANA, on premise, Cloud, private, public?

entry.title: Re: Enabling automatic document Split for an Outgoing Invoice with 999+ Items while posting an Accou
entry.content: I followed this implementation of METHOD FI_BILL_ISSUE_SPLIT~ACTIVATE_AUTOMATIC_SPLIT and   METHOD FI_BILL_ISSUE_SPLIT~SET_DOCUMENT_TYPE_SUBSEQ but I got this error on my end after I release to Accounting.

entry.title: How to setup Workflow for Enterprise Project
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

How can I setup workflow for Enterprise Project ?

I have setup it already but it doesn't work for Project Control - Enterprise Projects.

Best regards,

Keratiya D.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud: configuration, performance, sizing
entry.content:
SreekanthSurampally

1. Does APRS only configured and used for SAP managed data products? or can this be used in Custom Data products from Z CDS views in S/4 HANA to Datasphere object store?

2. HDLF as the target mentioned in this blog,  is not the one in Datasphere HDLF right?

3. In this SAP managed data products integration using APRS, Datasphere replication flows are not used right?

4. When Intelligent/Insight app is activated, the APRS initial and delta process is initiated from S/4 and load the data into HDLF of BDC Foundation service, then why an integration Datasphere space is created with replication flows to load the data for these data products?

1. In this blog APRS described  as part of BDC product. So, as I know currently BDC could replicated only SAP provided data products. Better refer to BDC documentation.

2. BUT, APRS also used in Datasphere when source is S/4 HANA system (target BDC-specific part in such case is not relevant). So, from this point of view, configuration and optimization of source system is the same.

3. As I mentioned in (2) Datasphere also used APRS when source is S/4 HANA system

4. Datasphere and BDC is 2 different SAP products. In both cases APRS used on source side. The subscription process is different and follow up data processing in both cases also difference.

entry.title: The results of the data action "XXXXXXX" could not be published on version "yyyy".
entry.content:
Hello, 

One user getting below error message when publish the data action from story. The data action is having 3 steps which copy data from different model.

All steps are running fine, but when publish step comes then it shows below error

The results of the data action "XXXXXXX" could not be published on version "yyyy". You don't have the resources available to publish this version. Please try again later." 

The strange part is that, when I run the data action I am able to do it without any issue. I am the owner of that model.

There is a KBA from SAP, but I am not getting as what to change here because I am able to run the data action without any issue.

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003618840

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud: configuration, performance, sizing
entry.content:
albertosimeoni
can APRS be used to replicate data outside SAP / HDLF ? 

APRS is not separate standalone product. It's used as part of numbers other products: SLT, DataIntelligence, DataSphere, BDC. In all cases where product replicate data from CDS View or SAP Table from S/4 HANA system.

entry.title: Re: Rise With SAP - The FUE Game
entry.content: None of these appear to relate to MIGO (not directly at least)? Which auth object is the issue?

entry.title: Re: Wrong behaviour of cl_gui_alv_grid or wrong usage?
entry.content:
I do not agree. ALV is the UI layer. If you offer values in an F4 help and then trick the user with an error message, that's a wrong UI design. And that's implemented.
Now the special cases: 
If a possible value selected by the user does not fit in all combinations to the complete data set values, then of course an error message is required. But it is triggered by business logic. The generic UI logic cannot decide. 
If a possible value selected by the user never fits to the complete data set values, then the Business logic must pimp the value set offered by the value help.

entry.title: Clarification on TDS Threshold Configuration in SAP S/4HANA Public
entry.content:
We would like to confirm whether TDS threshold-based deduction (as per Indian statutory requirements) can be configured and automated in our SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud environment.

Specifically, we are looking to configure:

  • Minimum invoice-level threshold (e.g., ₹30,000)

  • Cumulative yearly threshold (e.g., ₹1,00,000 per vendor per financial year)

  • Automatic TDS deduction once thresholds are breached

  • TDS applicability based on section-wise rates (e.g., 194C, 194J, etc.)

Kindly confirm the below:

  1. Can these thresholds be maintained in the WHT Type/Code configuration in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

  2. Is cumulative threshold tracking supported for each vendor annually?

  3. Do we need to activate any additional scope items or business configurations?

  4. Could you share the list of relevant SSCUIs or apps for maintaining this setup?

Appreciate your support on this. Please let us know if any additional steps or approvals are required from our side.

entry.title: IEQUIPMENTATTR cds view does not load delta properly
entry.content:
Hello Guys,

I've a problem to make delta extraction on the CDS view IEQUIPMENTATTR on my datasource 0I_EQUIPMENTDATA.

 

 

 

 

 

 

When I load full load everything seems to work fine but when I run it in delta mode it runs on error.

It says that no more data available to load but since it is an init it should load more rows definitely.

It seems to me that it runs on error on the source side but can’t seems to find any issue.
Could you please propose some solution/checks that would solve the issue ?

Thanks for all the hints help

entry.title: Re: User SAPSR3DB does not exist during an SAP Netweaver...
entry.content:
SWPM can do the ORACLE installation by itself on an automated way. 

But, if ORACLE DB is installed manually before the SWPM, then you need to perform a extended list of customizations as recommended by SAP.

Please look into the SAP official documentation: 

  • 2799900 - Central Technical Note for Oracle Database 19c - SAP for Me
  • 2660018 - Oracle Database Software Installation on Microsoft Windows - SAP for Me
  • Installation of SAP Systems Based on the Application Server ABAP of SAP NetWeaver 7.3 EHP1 to 7.52 on Windows: Oracle

entry.title: SAP BTP Web IDEの画面に表示されているJava Build Pack1廃止の警告の件について
entry.content:
各位
お疲れ様です。
ご存じの方がいらっしゃればなにとぞご教授ください。

SAP BTP Web IDEのJava Build Pack1が廃止されるという話が以下のブログに記載されていました。
Deprecation of Java Tools in SAP Web IDE 

今日が廃止対象の6月末ではあるのですが、こちらは以下の認識で合っておりますでしょうか？
・Cloud Foundryへのビルドができない
・現在すでにCloud Foundryへビルドされているものが動作しなくなるわけではない

また、Web IDE上の警告で以下の警告になっておりました。
ブログ記載の通り6月末が対象かと思っていたのですが、期間が変更になったということなのでしょうか？

原文：
Please note that on September 2025, java tools in sap web ide will be removed due to discontinuation of the java buildpack1 from the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment. For more information, see the Deprecation of Java Tools in SAP Web IDE blog post.

翻訳：
2025年9月、SAP BTP Cloud Foundry環境におけるJavaビルドパック1のサポート終了に伴い、SAP Web IDEのJavaツールも削除されますのでご注意ください。詳しくは、ブログ記事「SAP Web IDEにおけるJavaツールの廃止」をご覧ください。

ご協力よろしくお願いいたします。
#SAPBTP
#BuildPack1
#SAPWebIDE

entry.title: Re: ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud: configuration, performance, sizing
entry.content:
Sorry but this do not answer my question:

ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud introduces a new technology called ABAP Push Replication Service (APRS). APRS pushes the data of CDS views, which are part of Data Products, into HANA Data Lake Files (HDLFS).

From What I understand replication flows (used in normal spaces of Datasphere) do use APE (Abap Pipeline engine).
in that case is the replication flow that read from S/4 source system, it reads the RDB tables (PULL).

Due to limitations of RFC/ODP/ reserved API for SAP, external tools can not extract CDS views in init+delta mode (we know OData exists but it is slow and with some bugs, for example datetime columns not supported / crash errors).

In APRS: is the source applicaton that do a PUSH of data to HDLF? (no SAP private API call from external tool involved?)

Can APRS be used to push data to another object store ? (non SAP managed object Store?).

Thanks,
Alberto

entry.title: SAP public cloud edition. CO assigment is not available for mass update in posting area 2610
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I'm trying to add the COKEY CO Acc assignment field in the configuration but this field is not available for a mass update in posting area 2610 in SAP public cloud edition...

Any suggestion to update the posting area 2610 in a mass way?

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Problem activating Support user via SLD or RSP
entry.content:
We encounter the same problem. After activating the support user in SLD we cannot login with the support user in SLD.

The listed SAP Notes are not accessible yet. Do you have any updates on this matter?

entry.title: Re: Integrate Commerce cloud to multiple accounts of Emarsys
entry.content: Unfortunately it is not possible as Emarsys integration plugin checks whether there is already existing integration with target Commerce Cloud environment and do not allow to make another integration.

entry.title: How to load Data From ECC to S4 Hana Finance for Group Reporting
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are implementing GR for Priviate Edition. GR system is not yet ready, how ever we need to show one live cycle to customer. our source system is ECC and NoN integrated sytems(units). 

My questin is: How can I get the data from ECC to GR system.? is ther any program or methodology / procesdure to place the data into Group reporting system. 

Thanks,
MS Rao.

entry.title: Field ID is empty in Tax Category of TaxSubtotal
entry.content:
Hi,

Kindly how could I maintain Field ID in  Tax Category of TaxSubtotal? Is this in /n/AIF/VMAP -> Define Value Mapping? If yes, what could I maintain?

BR

entry.title: Re: Output tables in Kmeans
entry.content: @ChristophMorgen could you please provide me with more information on this? How can I delete temporary tables if they are not saved?

entry.title: Re: CDS : How to define Hierarchy View
entry.content: Yes we can use it on Prem also. S/4 HANA AP 2023.

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud release June 2025 Round-up
entry.content:
Dear SAP HANA Cloud Enthusiasts,

We are thrilled to share a comprehensive collection of the latest videos, blogposts, and resources from the SAP HANA Cloud Q2 2025 release.

 

What’s New Teaser

Explore the latest innovations in SAP HANA Cloud with Lead Product Manager Thomas Hammer! In this exciting teaser, he shares his favorite features from the newest release.

Watch it now on YouTube.

What’s New blogpost

Intrigued by the teaser? Explore our "What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud in June 2025" blog post for an in-depth look and find valuable links for further insights.

Read it here!

What's New Webinar

Prefer watching over reading? Our 'What’s New' webinar is just for you! Join our Product experts for an in-depth view of the latest features. Watch anytime here!

 

Additional information


In addition to our regular release updates, make sure to check the following valuable content provided by our team:

  • New Machine Learning and AI features in SAP HANA Cloud 2025 Q2 by @ChristophMorgen 
  • SAP HANA Cloud Availability in SAP Sovereign Cloud and SAP NS2 by @isomesan
  • Explore more on Calculation View features in this blogpost by @jan_zwickel 
Don’t miss out on all the content and remember to follow us in the SAP HANA Community.

Remember to check our content following the # whatsnewinsaphanacloud tag: here

Don’t forget to subscribe and follow SAP HANA Cloud on YouTube to always stay up-to-date regarding the most recent innovations in SAP HANA Cloud.
 
All the best,

Andrea on behalf of the SAP HANA Cloud team

#SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database   #SAP HANA Cloud  

#whatsnewinsaphanacloud

entry.title: Re: How to activate Transaction Builder in SAP MDK ( not...
entry.content:
Hello

You can simply set TransactionID in the RequestOptions of CreateEntity/UpdateEntity/DeleteEntity to group multiple independent operations into a Change Set without using the ChangeSet action. There should be no need to enable Transaction Builder in this case.

A change set will always be sent as a batch, but because your operations will be grouped in a change set, technically, if one of the operation failed, your backend will (or should) rollback all prior successful operations that’s in the same change set request that’s being executed.

Regards

Ming

entry.title: Re: Custom tokens in Onboarding
entry.content:
Hi Karthick,

Thanks for your response, I appreciate it.

One clarification question. I added fields for the custom roles in Onboarding Process Object, but when I get to the mapping I don't see a way the pick values I just added into via direct mapping, which would be my desire.

I think I am missing something, but I am not sure what.

Regards,

Piotr

entry.title: Re: Error when upload LTMC PIR with condition in SAP Public Cloud

For more details on how to use tags can be found in Blog https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/q-amp-a-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-how-to-get-your-answer-faster/ba-p/13934481

entry.title: Duplicate file check in sFTP adapter BTP cloud integration
entry.content:
Hello all,

In SAP PI world we had duplicate file check option at adapter level for both NFS and sFTP.

We are trying to achieve the same thing in sFTP adapter in CPI but we do not have direct option available.

If anyone has done this scenario, can you please help us how to achieve this in CPI.

entry.title: Re: How to setup Workflow for Enterprise Project
entry.content:
Hello  @Keratiya_D 

Thanks for your question. The Manage Workflows for Enterprise Project app in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition currently supports workflows for Enterprise Project creation or changes, but it does not control processes inside the Project Control - Enterprise Projects app.

The two apps operate separately.
Workflow setup applies during project master data creation or change approval
It doesn’t trigger workflows for specific activities in Project Control

If you need workflow for Project Control actions, that functionality isn’t available yet in standard Public Cloud, it would require an SAP Influence Request for future roadmap consideration.

Check the SAP Help Reference:
SAP Help – Workflows for Enterprise Projects

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Principal Propagation Authentication to S/4HANA Backend without Cloud Connector
entry.content:
At one of our RISE w SAP customers we have the following request open:

Single Sign-On requests coming from SAP Event Mesh / Build Work Zone, etc which come from public endpoints, cannot go over the SAP Cloud Connector via Principal Propagation, but via an internal gateway system. 

What are the possibilities to authenticate to S/4HANA ABAP backend, so we can use the odata services, etc? Is OpenId an option? We cannot just add the authentication header in the request of the API, as this is insecure.

Thanks for your feedback!

entry.title: Re: Add header text to uploading budget template PSM
entry.content: Hi BAKKET, Thank you for joining the community. I have informed the potential expert in this area for answering your question. Best Regards, Irene

entry.title: Re: Securing Data Journey with SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content: Great blog indeed, very insightful... thanks Jana!!

entry.title: Fetch table content from a webpage in sap build process automation
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Need your expert's suggestion,

I’m building an automation in sap build process automation that needs to pull the exchange‑rate table from our bank’s website and update those rates into SAP S/4HANA.

Bank is not providing any API to fetch exchange rate.

As of now, I am using "Execute Command" to open a website and using keystroke to select the content of webpage.

Now I have to fetch exchange rate table from bank website and update the same in S/4 Hana, I tried multiple possible way like custom script, but somehow, I am not able to fetch the information. Kindly help.

entry.title: how to display attachments like (docx and pdf) based on form id in adobe forms

entry.title: Faster Backups with Db2 Intra-Tablespace Parallelism for SAP Applications
entry.content:
With Db2 12.1, intra-tablespace parallelism (ITP) for backups was introduced. This new feature addresses long backup runtimes of databases dominated by a single or few large tablespaces. In a lab test at IBM, we examined what you can gain through this new capability.
For SAP systems on IBM Db2 for Linux, UNIX, and Windows (Db2 for LUW), tablespace pools are used to create a balanced tablespace layout. But even with tablespace pools, you can’t always prevent tablespaces from growing more than others.
In this blog post, I’m going to share my findings with you. But first, let me give you some more background information about ITP.

Why ITP?

Intra-tablespace parallelism enhances the Db2 parallelism options for backup operations. It’s enabled by default, but you can easily disable it. Simply set the registry variable DB2_BACKUP_ITP to OFF.

Background

Before Db2 12.1, the Db2 backup process assigns a maximum of one Db2 buffer manipulator (db2bm) EDU (engine dispatchable unit) to a tablespace. The first db2bm EDU is assigned to the largest tablespace, then to the second largest, and so on in descending order. The value of the backup parallelization option defines the maximum of db2bm EDUs that are used.
This mechanism scales very well if there are many tablespaces and if the tablespaces are all equally sized.
For databases where one or a few tablespaces are extremely large and the remaining tablespaces are rather small, the overall backup runtime is typically determined by the largest tablespace.

How does ITP work?

Intra-tablespace parallelism (ITP) for backups addresses the above-mentioned database layout challenges. With the ITP feature, tablespaces are logically split into smaller pieces for the backup process. These pieces, called segments, can now be assigned to db2bm EDUs for reading the data. A large tablespace is now accessed by more than one buffer manipulator which speeds up the backup process of this tablespace significantly.
ITP also improves the runtime of compressed and encrypted backups because the workload of performing the compression and encryption can now be spread over multiple cores.

Note
When using ITP, you will only experience a backup runtime improvement if your system provides sufficient bandwidth in the I/O subsystem. Upgrading an existing database to Db2 12.1 automatically enables ITP for backups.

When to use ITP

In case you have an asymmetric tablespace layout with one or several very large tablespaces and a lot of small ones, you should check the backup performance statistics of your recent backups (BARstats) in the db2diag.log. (Don’t’ worry, the BARstats are explained in detail later in this blog post.)
An indicator that ITP can help reduce the overall backup time is the uneven distribution of entries in the performance statistics, like I/O or Mbytes. If you have such an uneven distribution in the performance statistics and your system is not I/O bound, you will most probably benefit from the ITP feature with shorter backup runtimes.

Setup of the test system

Let’s have a look at the results of the lab test at IBM where ITP influenced the backup runtime positively.
The main components of our test environment were:

• POWER LPAR: 8 dedicated physical CPUs, SMT4, 16GB memory
• AIX: 7200-05-07
• IBM disk storage: database
• IBM SSD storage: backup target
• Db2: 12.1
• Database size: 468GB
• Number of tablespaces: 58
• SAP ERP system

The tests were run as offline backups with no additional load on the AIX LPAR. The database was set up with an asymmetric tablespace layout in such a way that the largest tablespace occupied about 263GB and represented 56% of the overall database size. The following chart shows the size of the first 19 out of 58 tablespaces in descending order:

 

Figure 1: Tablespace layout of the backup environment in descending order

To avoid an overload of the test system’s I/O infrastructure, Db2 backup compression was enabled. The backup parallelization value and the backup targets were defined via the backup command. We used a backup parallelization of four and two targets:

db2 "backup db SRC to <backup target1, backup target 2> with 8 buffers parallelism 4 compress"

Eventually, we ran two backups: The first one with ITP disabled, and the second one with ITP enabled and an ITP parallelization of four (automatically chosen).

Results: Let’s talk numbers

To compare the behavior of the two backups, let’s first have a look at the BARstats entries in the db2diag.log. The tables below are an excerpt of the BARstats with the columns needed for our analysis.

BARstats: ITP disabled

Let’s start with the BARstats for a backup where the ITP feature is disabled. In our case, there are five columns that indicate that we have an asymmetric tablespace layout, and ITP may reduce the overall backup runtime: I/O, Compr, WaitQ, Buffers, and Mbytes.

 

BM#TotalI/OComprMsgQWaitQThrottleBuffersMBytes
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
0001:37:590:07:031:30:120:00:000:00:000:00:0016778269690
0011:37:590:01:510:20:130:00:001:15:520:00:00226365676
0021:37:590:02:000:20:040:00:001:15:520:00:00194571132
0031:37:590:02:040:20:010:00:001:15:520:00:00213271909
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TOT------23118 478409
Table 1: ITP disabled - Backup Performance Statistic (BARstats) of an asymmetric tablespace layout.

The first column of interest is the I/O column which lists the time the individual db2bm EDUs spent for reading data from the storage infrastructure. The db2bm EDU BM#000 is active for about seven minutes whereas the other three are only active for about 2 minutes.
The Compr column shows that these three db2bm EDUs were active for about 20 minutes to compress data for the backup, whereas the db2bm EDU BM#000 did most of the work and was active for about 1h and 30 minutes . In summary, these three EDUs were most of the time idle as the entries in the WaitQ column show in accordance with the Compr and I/O column.
The last two columns in the table (Buffers and Mbytes) describe the same behavior merely from the amount of transferred data or used buffers. The db2bm EDU BM#000 needs to transfer about 270GB of data (the complete data of the largest tablespace) whereas the three remaining EDUs only transfer about 70GB each for all other tablespaces.
With ITP disabled, a db2bm EDU is only able to back up one tablespace. Therefore, three db2bm EDUs are idle most of the time after they saved about 44 percent of the database data.
The db2bm EDU BM#000 needs to back up about 56 percent of the data – the largest tablespace - and so determines the overall backup runtime.
Finally, this system behavior leads to a backup runtime of 1h 37m 59s. The backup runtime is represented by the column “Total” in table 1.

Now let’s check how the backup runtime changes after we enable the ITP feature.

BARstats: ITP enabled

After ITP is enabled, the uneven I/O distribution over the db2bm EDUs disappears and the overall backup runtime reduces significantly to about 44 minutes as shown in column “Total”.

BM#TotalI/OComprMsgQWaitQThrottleBuffersMBytes
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
0000:44:140:04:220:39:370:00:000:00:000:00:005800117628
0010:44:140:04:320:39:290:00:000:00:000:00:005792121651
0020:44:140:04:260:39:330:00:000:00:000:00:005928118155
0030:44:140:04:270:39:320:00:000:00:000:00:005598120973
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TOT------23118 478409
Table 2: ITP enabled - Backup Performance Statistic (BARstats) of an asymmetric tablespace layout

All db2bm EDUs now spent the same amount of time reading data, about 4m 30s as displayed in the I/O column. Also, the unevenness of the entries in the other columns Compr, WaitQ, Buffers and MBytes vanishes. The db2bm EDUs now use the same amount of time for compressing the data (Compr column), the same number of buffers (Buffers column), and handle the same amount of data (MBytes column). The three entries in the WaitQ column are reduced to zero because the three db2bm EDUs are not waiting for db2bm EDU BM#000 anymore.

Due to the split of the tablespaces into smaller segments, all db2bm EDUs can now backup data from the largest tablespace. All four db2bm EDUs are now equally used and back up nearly the same amount of data. Compared to the previous BARstats db2bm EDU BM#001, BM#002 and BM#003 nearly doubled the processed data from about 70GBytes to 120GBytes.

CPU, disk I/O and runtime comparison

After comparing the BARstats entries for the two backups, let’s now check the CPU and disk I/O resource usage.

 

 

 
 
Figure 2: Disk and CPU consumption for backups with asymmetric tablespace layout without ITP

Figure 2 shows that the backup with ITP disabled can use the four db2bm EDUs only at the beginning until about 1440s of the total runtime. From there, only one db2bm EDU keeps working on the data of the largest tablespace. At that point, the I/O and CPU consumption drops to about 25% of the previously used resources (from about 27% to about 7% for read hdisk6) because the available CPU and I/O resources can’t be fully used anymore, and the backup runs for 5870 seconds.
However, with ITP enabled, the work is distributed evenly over all four db2bm EDUs from the beginning as figure 3 shows. All CPU and disk I/O resources are used over the complete time range very efficiently, and therefore the backup runtime is reduced to 2654 seconds.

 

 

 
Figure 3: Disk and CPU consumption for backups with asymmetric tablespace layout with ITP

Benefits with ITP

Table 3 shows how advantageous the usage of ITP backup was for our test system with one very large tablespace that contained more than 50% of all data. The overall runtime shrinks from 5879 seconds to 2654 seconds – only 45 percent of the original runtime.

Backup TypeITP OFFITP ON
Total Backup Runtime in [s]58792654
Backup Runtime in [%]100%45%
Table 3: Backup runtime comparison with and without ITP

Behind the scenes

The next two tables (no. 4 and 5) show how the tablespaces (TS) are processed by the four db2bm EDUs that are responsible for reading data from disk. For comparison, have a look again at figure 1 from the beginning that shows the sizes of the largest tablespaces.

Table 4 presents the schedule for the backup with ITP disabled. Here we start the backup with reading the data from the four largest tablespaces. Since one db2bm EDU can handle only one tablespace at a time, the largest tablespace (SRC#DATA@20L), which is processed by the db2bm EDU 0, is active during the complete backup runtime. The other three db2bm EDUs process the remaining tablespaces one after the other.

These three db2bm EDUs finish nearly at the same time after about 22 minutes of the total backup runtime of 97 minutes and are then idle until the backup finishes.

Table 4: Schedule of the tablespaces handled by the db2bm EDUs – with ITP disabled

Table 5 presents the schedule for the backup with ITP enabled - by default starting with Db2 12.1. Since we enabled ITP, the tablespaces are now logically split into smaller pieces called segments. Each of these segments can now be handled by a db2bm EDU.

As you can see below, the backup is still starting with the largest tablespaces, but now all db2bm EDUs can work on the same tablespace at the same time, which is shown below for tablespace SRC#DATA@20L.

In this case, the largest tablespace SRC#DATA@20L is the first tablespace completely saved. Nearly all segments of the four largest tablespaces are saved before the db2bm EDUs start working on the smaller tablespaces – in our case SRC#DATA@03L. At the end of the backup, all four db2bm EDUs finish their work at the same time and none of them was idle.

Table 5: Schedule of the tablespaces handled by the db2bm EDUs – with ITP enabled

Table 6 shows the number of segments of the four largest tablespaces that are saved before the backup of a smaller tablespace starts (SRC#DATA@03L). At that point in time, the largest tablespace with its 9 segments is completely saved and so are 28 of 36 segments of the four largest ones. As you can see, large tablespaces are prioritized by the scheduler.

Table 6: Number of segments already stored in the backup when the fifth largest tablespace starts getting saved.

What about restore?

Now that I’ve shown you that it can be beneficial to use ITP backups for databases with one or a few large tablespaces, the question remains: Are there any drawbacks for the case when backups are made for a database restore?
The short answer is no. In our lab test environment, there was no runtime difference for restoring a backup made with or without ITP enabled. In both cases, the restore took us about 2200 seconds.

Conclusion

The feature intra-tablespace parallelism (ITP) is by default enabled for Db2 versions as of 12.1. It’s for database implementations that are dominated by one or few very large tablespaces. Such implementations always have issues with long backup runtimes caused by the large tablespace(s). Because as of Db2 12.1, the new ITP feature splits all tablespaces logically into smaller segments, the backup runtime will shrink.
In the IBM lab environment, we found that the backup runtime of our database with an asymmetric tablespace layout decreased significantly. In our case, with the largest tablespace representing about 56 % of the whole database, we achieved a backup runtime reduction up to 45% of the previous runtime. Keep in mind that the system needs free I/O capacity to leverage the new backup parallelization successfully.

What do you think of the ITP feature? Have you tried it yourself? Let me know in the comments.

entry.title: Re: Create a Data Collection with ScriptTask and Log_DC API
entry.content: Hi Kevin, hi Steve, how did you prepare your JSON structure to fill out multiple parameters at once?

entry.title: Filter on Employee centeral one time payment
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m working on a scenario involving SAP SuccessFactors One-Time Payments, and I’d appreciate your insights on a potential workaround we're evaluating.

Context

  • The customer maintains an Assignment Number in SAP HCM IT0015.

  • This Assignment Number will be replicated from HCM to Employee Central, and populated into the customString1 field of the EmpPayCompNonRecurring entity.

  • The customer uses this Assignment Number to group multiple one-time payments.

  • A custom application reads one-time payment records from SuccessFactors, using this Assignment Number as the key for grouping.

The Problem

  • In the EmpPayCompNonRecurring entity, the field customString1 is not filterable via the OData API.

  • This limitation prevents us from using queries like:

    http
     
    $filter=customString1 eq '10000069'

  • The only available field that is filterable in this entity is sequenceNumber, which is a standard field.

Proposed Workaround

  • Create a business rule on the One-Time Payment “onSave” event.

  • Use this rule to populate the sequenceNumber field with:

    • The Assignment Number (from customString1),

    • Concatenated with a unique identifier to ensure each record remains distinct.

  • This would enable OData queries like:

    http
     
    $filter=startswith(sequenceNumber, '10000069')

    which would effectively retrieve all one-time payments grouped by Assignment Number.

Customer Concern

  • The customer is hesitant to use sequenceNumber in this way, since it is a standard field.

  • They are concerned that modifying it could introduce side effects or conflict with existing or future system behaviour.

Although I haven't found any technical issues with this approach so far, the concern is valid and understandable.

Questions for the Community

  • Has anyone implemented something similar to group or filter one-time payments using Assignment Number?

  • Have you ever overridden sequenceNumber for custom logic?

  • Are there any known side effects or caveats of using(modifying) standard fields like sequenceNumber this way?

  • Should we consider raising a ticket to SAP requesting enhancement of the One-Time Payment OData API to make fields like customString1 and customDouble1 filterable?

Looking forward to your thoughts and recommendations!

Thanks in advance,
Manu

entry.title: Re: Solved - How to join QUT1 and QUT10 tables?
entry.content:
Hi Fabio,

I think you forgot the OrderNum in your VisOrder. It is possible that there is more than one Textline. So you have to order it, too

regards Lothar

entry.title: Re: Point Of View - SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2

entry.title: Re: Vertical scrollbar jumps to end.
entry.content:
Hi, when we click on check box, it calls handle event method where it manipulate checkbox (eg:- select or deselect based on other checkbox.....etc). Inside this method before manipulation, we are calling "get_channel_state" and after manipulation called "set_channel_state", this two method were called to get and set channel structure so that we can avoid collapse of alv tree. Now my doubt is weather scroll behaviour is controlled by standard or there is any method which control the behaviour of scrollbar or how it is associated with select/deselect of checkbox event??  @Sandra_Rossi Thank You.

Sample code snippet.

entry.title: Re: Vertical scrollbar jumps to end.
Re: Token Retrieval In CPI fails but works in postman
entry.content:
Hi Ajmaradiaga, I have used Auth2 client credentials as security material in CPI. But i am getting error: unresolved variable : request.formparam.code

In my  iflow i am calling a POST API as receiver in CPI. I have 2 steps.

First Content Modifier and then Request Reply. 

Can you share the iflow details? what all steps needed and what all we need to pass in each step?  

Thanks

Gopal

entry.title: Re: Duplicate file check in sFTP adapter BTP cloud integr...
entry.content: Either you have to set a lookup in CPI to sort of check where the list of file names are recorded or just let the files through and have ABAP do the duplicate check for processing. We did the latter on my previous project.

entry.title: sapgui 800 Patch 6 - error 12007 despite xml file is accessible
entry.content:
hi all,

we have rolled out SAPGUI 800 PL 6 and getting reports from some users that when they start the SAPGUI app, they will get an error stating that the centrally placed xml config file cannot be read, despite there is no issue with reaching the xml file from the affected user machine. In most cases, when confirming the error message with "OK" , all is working as expected and systems are shown in the system overview section.

Does anyone else have a similar issue and an idea on how to get this resolved? During the testing this issue was not captured,  and I cannot find anything related to that in any SAPnotes (in regards to error 12007 and sapgui 800).

Any help is highly appreciated.

Best regards

alex

entry.title: Re: The results of the data action "XXXXXXX" could not be...
entry.content:
@saikhancs3524 

The error comes when the tenant does not have enough memory available to publish the data records generated by the Data Action. Happens when Data Action created lot of data records. You could try few steps below

  • Try running the Data Action for a limited scope. For e.g. 1 month or 1 company code etc.
  • Assign the correct planning area settings for the Data Action. You could use Empty Planning Area and see if that works. You can find the option on right pane of the Data Action starter settings.

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Sending notifications inside Fiori after using send object with note
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
In T-code me23n when "send object with note" is used, notification is sent in sap business workplace.
How can this notification be sent in my inbox or push notification in Fiori as well?

entry.title: Re: Unable to deploy value map - Error: Select default va...
entry.content: you have a source value with at least two target value in the value mapping. that is what's happening so you're asked to define a default value.

entry.title: external definition imported from sap pi is causing issue in sap cpi
entry.content:
hello,

we are migrating sap pi interfaces to sap cpi. after migrating interfaces which had external definition used in sap pi for source or target structure, we see in sap cpi in message mapping the structure shows missing node .may be due to prefix?

Is there any standard solution to fix this kind of issues as we have many pi interfaces to migrate to cpi and most o them use external definition in sap pi

 

thanks,

Sahana

entry.title: Issue with Time Event Pairing "P01" Over Weekends in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello,

We are currently encountering an issue related to time event pairing in SuccessFactors.

Our terminal system only sends "P01" time events to SuccessFactors, which requires that each event be paired with another of the same time type. However, we are experiencing a problem during weekends: the last time event on Friday is not being paired correctly, as the next time event occurs more than 24 hours later (typically on Monday). This time gap prevents the system from recognizing and pairing the events properly.

To address this, we considered creating a business rule that would automatically generate a manual time event (specifically a stop event) to close the open pair. However, we have not been successful in implementing this solution so far.

Has anyone faced a similar issue, or does anyone have suggestions or best practices for resolving this?

We would greatly appreciate your guidance or recommendations.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: New SAP Build Process Automation content for side-by-side extensions with flexible workflows
entry.content:
My colleagues published a SAP Build Process Automation action package "Flexible Workflow Scenario Notifications Action" on SAP Business Accelerator Hub and in the SAP Build Store so that it becomes easier for Citizen Developer to discover and consume it.

It oﬀers an action for an outcome driven extension workﬂow and makes it easier to combine ﬂexible workﬂows from SAP S/4HANA with SAP Build Process Automation capabilities.

From a use case perspective, you could think of including process participants from outside (like external project managers) into the SAP S/4HANA Process. Or you include centrally managed services from SAP BTP like a Rules Service or Joule Skills into your SAP S/4HANA workﬂow project. Or you like to leverage ML services like the recommendation capability of SAP Task Center, which is only available for SAP Build Process Automation tasks? For the blog I took the use case with process participants from outside to explain the possibilities.

In line with side-by-side extensibility ﬂexible workﬂow oﬀers an activitytype "extension step" which can be implemented by flexible workflow scenarios to offer side-by-side extension. It is guideline for all scenario developers to enable it.

I will give a short overview and add some points in addition to the oﬃcial documentation.

The solution architecture gives an overview on the connection between Sales Order Creation in SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP. Over the ﬂexible workﬂow capability of SAP Business Workﬂow it is possible to start an extension process and get back the result / outcome from SAP Build Process Automation .

Configuration in SAP S/4HANA starts with definition of decision reasons for sales orders. In our use case we want to start a flexible workﬂow which is the internal approval solution. After setting an internal approval reason a BAdI checks after saving the Sales Order if an approval is necessary. If true a start event is thrown and checks if a workﬂow is deﬁned in the Manage Workﬂow Application for our scenario. If the scenario contains an extension step, the business process expert is able to configures an extension step with a process deﬁnition from SAP Build Process Automation from the connected BTP subaccount. During runtime when the flow comes to the extension, a SAP Build Process Automation workflow is started via api and a standardize payload.

In the extension workﬂow of our use case we derive the complete sales order header data over an API call to SAP S/4HANA and use this data for Agent Determination. After agent determination via decision table a user decision is modelled with two outcomes.

After the user decision took place we need to set the outcome via script and use it in the action content package. Go to SAP Build Store. Search for "flexible".

Click on the action project for review the details.

Add the action to your project.

To use the action and point to the correct ABAP-Backend-System:

  1. define a destination variable in the SAP Build Process Automation project.
  2. define a destination in the BTP Cockpit to the ABAP-Backend-System(s)
  3. map the destination to the destination variable during deployment of the project.

Define the workflowInstanceId in the context and fill it from the start payload. For the names of the outcomes you need to look up the flexible workflow scenario or check the documentation of the used scenario. If it is custom build in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition or SAP S/4HANA (like in this blog) check your implementation of the extension step and fill the outcomes accordingly.

Please be aware that the development livecycle of the projects on SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP is independent. We work on further enhancements to simplify the integration via the extensibility wizard and also on more features to enrich the possibilities. So stay tuned. A big thanks to all colleagues supporting the integration.

entry.title: Re: Manage Material Valuations App on S/4HANA Cloud, Publ...
entry.content:
Hi Owen,

Thanks for your comment.  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/331439/ is the request for customer influence, asking to vote.

BR

entry.title: Re: Point Of View - SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2
entry.content:
Great Post @SujitWani4 👏
#welldone

entry.title: SAP BTP SDK for iOS incompatibility issues with Swift 6 (Xcode 16)
entry.content:
Hi SAP community.

I am facing some issues with the SAP SDK with Swift 6. Swift 6 is mainly about concurrency changes in Swift which does not seem to play along with the SAPFoundation XCFramework.

It seems like the SAPFoundation framework internally manages its own dispatch queues (specifically the SAP.Foundation.Authentication.WebViewAuthenticator.SingleAccess queue).

If a function uses this queue and is called within a MainActor or Task context, no matter if it is isolated or not, this will cause a crash.

An example of this is the following method:
SAMLAuthenticator.authenticate(session: _, completionHandler: _)

To reproduce the issue one can enable the Swift 6 language mode, initialize a SAMLAuthenticator and try to call authenticate in a Task { … } context or from a @MainActor context.

SAP BTP SDK version: 25.4.4
Xcode version: 16.4

Are there any known solutions to this issue?

entry.title: Custom Header in CPI
entry.content:
Hi, I added a script in my iFlow to add custom headers in MPL - I'm using the standard script however the custom header still shows in the message log even though the property has no value or equals to null. 

 The "po_property" stores a value from a local variable (which I deleted in this scenario) so basically it's considered as null? (correct me if I'm wrong)

Here's my script:

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;

def Message processData(Message message) {
    
	def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message);
	if(messageLog != null){

		def po_number = message.getHeaders().get("po_number");		
		if(po_number!=null){
			messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty("Local Variable Test", po_number);		
        }
	}
	return message;
}

Has anyone encountered this or did I miss something?

entry.title: Logic for calculation of the actual coverage in the "Period total" view of MD04 screen
entry.content:
Hi All,
I want to understand the system logic of calculating the actual coverage in the "Period total" view of MD04 screen.
There is no coverage profile assigned in material master. If no coverage profile is assigned in material master how it calculates actual coverage which I am able to see in - MD04 -> Period Totals -> Actual Coverage (In days).

For Example:

Requirements-525.400,000-

Available quantity- 288.012,021

Actual coverage - 15,5

 

Requirements-58.228,000-

Available quantity- 578.188,021

Actual coverage - 34,2

Please let me know if you have any information.
Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Comparing Two XML Element Groups Using XSLT: A Practical Approach for Data Synchronization
entry.content: Seems your intention is to make a blog post. However, you have posted this a Question. go back to https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/bg-p/technology-blog-members is where you make a blog post

entry.title: GRC BRM - Digital signature enablement Option is not seen
entry.content:
Dear SAP community, We are currently implementing BRM (Business Role Management) for a client and are looking to enable digital signature functionality during workflow approvals — specifically, we require approvers to enter their user ID and password when approving requests in their work inbox (similar to how it functions in ARM - Access Request Management).

However, we are unable to locate the "Digital Signature Enablement" option within BRM configuration. Could you please advise on how we can achieve the similar functionality in BRM. Please share the SAP note or configuration steps if available.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to physically delete all development objects assigned to one package from the system?
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have a similar requirement: I need to delete several objects that begin with a specific namespace and then transport them. The challenge is that there are approximately 10,000 objects in TADIR with this namespace.  

Can anyone suggest an efficient and reliable approach to handle this?

 

Thanks,
Smriti

entry.title: Re: Output tables in Kmeans
entry.content:
@Frengo, it is somewhat not transparent to me / the community how your app / scenarios looks in detail, which tools you use etc.

From the error code you shared, one idea about you problem is that in your procedure code, you use references to temporary tables which might not exist anymore by the time you execute the procedure: Can not insert data from temp table

entry.title: How to use OBH1/OBH2 on SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear Experts.

I have a lot of companies, document types.

I want to use Tr-cd:OBH1/OBH2 on SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition.

If I can't these PG, I can't maintain the number range intervals.

Please help.
